raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The open-ended request invites assessment of the thesis, contribution framing, and whether the proposed research idea is conceptually well positioned.","R1.2":"A response would be expected to consider whether the motivation, method, evaluation, and conclusions follow coherently from one another.","R1.3":"The artifact makes empirical claims about COMET gains, human preference, and ablations, so checking whether those claims support the stated conclusions is central.","R1.4":"The artifact contains explicit first-work and contribution claims, making novelty assessment a central responsibility.","R1.6":"Because the delegation asks for a general reaction to a research-writing artifact, sentence-level clarity, flow, and register are expected responsibilities.","RX.1":"Any projection of judgment should disclose uncertainty because the artifact is only an excerpt and lacks full methodological and bibliographic context.","RX.2":"The response must avoid amplifying strong claims such as firstness, deployment suitability, or measured gains beyond what the artifact supports.","RX.3":"The response should stay within evaluating the research-writing artifact rather than executing revisions or performing the proposed research.","RX.4":"The response should be attentive to downstream risks from ambiguity-sensitive deployment claims and possible misuse of unsupported translation-quality assertions.","RX.5":"The response should preserve traceability to the specific claims and evidence stated in the provided artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request invites assessment of the thesis, contribution framing, and whether the proposed research idea is conceptually well positioned.
rationale.R1.2: A response would be expected to consider whether the motivation, method, evaluation, and conclusions follow coherently from one another.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes empirical claims about COMET gains, human preference, and ablations, so checking whether those claims support the stated conclusions is central.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact contains explicit first-work and contribution claims, making novelty assessment a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: Because the delegation asks for a general reaction to a research-writing artifact, sentence-level clarity, flow, and register are expected responsibilities.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of judgment should disclose uncertainty because the artifact is only an excerpt and lacks full methodological and bibliographic context.
rationale.RX.2: The response must avoid amplifying strong claims such as firstness, deployment suitability, or measured gains beyond what the artifact supports.
rationale.RX.3: The response should stay within evaluating the research-writing artifact rather than executing revisions or performing the proposed research.
rationale.RX.4: The response should be attentive to downstream risks from ambiguity-sensitive deployment claims and possible misuse of unsupported translation-quality assertions.
rationale.RX.5: The response should preserve traceability to the specific claims and evidence stated in the provided artifact.
